purpose: Register and evaluate named dependency probes so readiness and diagnostics surfaces can report which backing services are healthy, degraded, or unavailable.

endpoints.description: Return the current status of all registered dependency probes.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Expose the state of registered dependency probes for readiness and diagnostics.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use when a service depends on several internal or local subsystems and callers need a consolidated dependency health view.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a long-running remote monitor, alerting platform, or deployment orchestrator.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Register dependency probe functions. 2) Call /health/dependencies. 3) Use the returned statuses in readiness or diagnostics responses.

failure_modes.condition: Probe function throws or times out
failure_modes.symptom: Dependency status marked degraded or unavailable with a reason
failure_modes.recovery: Repair the dependency or adjust the probe implementation to return deterministic status data

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Store named probe callbacks or adapters in a registry, execute them with bounded timing, and normalize their results into healthy, degraded, or unavailable states.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Health endpoints become misleading when one slow probe blocks all others, so isolate probe execution and preserve partial results.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Return per-dependency status objects with names and reasons rather than one opaque aggregate boolean.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not hide probe failures behind a blanket healthy response.
